graph.metadata.vendor_name = "Google"
graph.metadata.product_name = "Google Threat Intelligence"
graph.additional.fields["threat_list_id"] = ""

markdown.content:
## GTI Threat Intelligence ![alt text](https://files.readme.io/2bd0d6e09e34d1a8075ca7c9fb7a1ff76a11f2636cef55fbcdc89017121f9724-gti-favicon-colored.svg)
---	
#### This dashboard shows GTI IOC Stream as normalized into the Google SecOps Entity Context Graph (ECG).
 
#### The IOC Stream dashboard provides detailed information about IOC Stream indicators.

## VPN, TOR AND PROXY IPs
---
#### An object representing IPs tagged as VPNs, Tor, and Proxy. The data is a subset of the IP Addresses.

query.query:
graph.metadata.vendor_name = "Google"
graph.metadata.product_name = "Google Threat Intelligence"
graph.additional.fields["threat_list_id"] = ""
graph.metadata.entity_type = "URL"
graph.metadata.product_entity_id = ${entityid}
$tags = graph.entity.url_metadata.tags
match:
    $tags

markdown.content:
## DOMAIN IOCS 
---
#### A Domain object encapsulates information pertaining to a specific domain or Fully Qualified Domain Name (FQDN).

graph.additional.fields["threat_list_id"] = ""
graph.metadata.entity_type = "DOMAIN_NAME"
graph.metadata.product_entity_id = ${entityid}

markdown.content:
## IP ADDRESS
---
#### An IP Address object encapsulates information about a specific IP address.

markdown.content:
## FILE IOCS 
---
#### File objects represent a fundamental component of the Google Threat Intelligence (GTI) platform. GTI maintains an extensive repository of over two billion files that have undergone detailed analysis. A file object can be acquired by uploading a new file for analysis, querying for an existing file hash, or through various other search functionalities within the service.

## URL IOCS 
---
#### A `URL` (Uniform Resource Locator) object is distinct from a Domain object in that it specifies the complete path to a particular resource, whereas a domain represents the top-level address (e.g., `www.example.com/page.html` VS. `example.com`). Within Google Threat Intelligence (GTI), `URLs` serve a dual purpose. They are not only analyzed as standalone entities but also provide crucial contextual information by linking to files, domains, and other indicators observed at that specific web location.

graph.metadata.vendor_name = "Google"

graph.additional.fields["threat_list_id"] = ""

dashboardQueries.name: a04b9a1a-a28e-4791-b063-7fbbc50a0aaa
dashboardQueries.query:
graph.metadata.vendor_name = "Google"
graph.metadata.product_name = "Google Threat Intelligence"
graph.additional.fields["threat_list_id"] = ""
graph.metadata.entity_type = "IP_ADDRESS"
graph.entity.artifact.tags = /.*vpn.*/ or graph.entity.artifact.tags = /.*tor.*/ or graph.entity.artifact.tags = /.*proxy.*/
$ingestedtime = timestamp.get_timestamp(graph.metadata.collected_timestamp.seconds, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
$verdict = graph.metadata.threat.threat_verdict
$entityid = graph.metadata.product_entity_id
$threatscore = graph.metadata.threat.risk_score
$severity = graph.metadata.threat.severity
$tag = graph.entity.artifact.tags
$as = if(graph.entity.artifact.as_owner!="",graph.entity.artifact.as_owner,"-")
$detections = graph.metadata.threat.verdict_info.malicious_count
$location = if(graph.entity.artifact.location.country_or_region!="",graph.entity.artifact.location.country_or_region,"-")
$network = if(graph.entity.artifact.network.ip_subnet_range!="", graph.entity.artifact.network.ip_subnet_range, "-")
match:
    $entityid, $verdict, $threatscore, $severity, $as, $detections, $location, $network
outcome:
    $time = window.last(cast.as_int($ingestedtime), $ingestedtime)
    $tags = re.replace((arrays.join_string(array_distinct(if($tag!="", $tag, "-")),",")), "(,-)|(-,)","")
order:
    $time desc
limit:
    10000

dashboardQueries.name: 95d9b0d7-c93c-44ad-a450-905477f0eb7e
dashboardQueries.query:

$owner = graph.entity.artifact.as_owner
$entityid = graph.metadata.product_entity_id
match:
    $owner
outcome:
    $count = count_distinct($entityid)
order:
    $count desc
limit:
    10

dashboardQueries.name: 0ed837da-60ac-44dc-a4b8-1a9c6ae4b9eb
dashboardQueries.query:
graph.metadata.vendor_name = "Google"
graph.metadata.product_name = "Google Threat Intelligence"
graph.additional.fields["threat_list_id"] = ""
graph.metadata.entity_type = "FILE"
$ingestedtime = timestamp.get_timestamp(graph.metadata.collected_timestamp.seconds, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
$verdict = graph.metadata.threat.threat_verdict
$entityid = graph.metadata.product_entity_id
$threatscore = graph.metadata.threat.risk_score
$severity = graph.metadata.threat.severity
$filetype = graph.entity.file.tags
$threatlabel = if(graph.additional.fields["popular_threat_classification_suggested_threat_label"]!="",graph.additional.fields["popular_threat_classification_suggested_threat_label"], "-")
$firstsubmissiontime = timestamp.get_timestamp(graph.entity.file.first_submission_time.seconds, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
$detections = graph.metadata.threat.verdict_info.malicious_count
$yararules = if(graph.additional.fields["crowdsourced_yara_results_count"]!="-",cast.as_string(cast.as_int(graph.additional.fields["crowdsourced_yara_results_count"]) + 1), "0")
$threatscore != 0
match:
    $entityid, $verdict, $threatscore, $severity, $firstsubmissiontime, $detections, $threatlabel, $yararules
outcome:
    $time = window.last(cast.as_int($ingestedtime), $ingestedtime)
    $filetypes = re.replace((arrays.join_string(array_distinct(if($filetype!="", $filetype, "-")),",")), "(,-)|(-,)","")
order:
    $time desc
limit:
    10000

dashboardQueries.name: f0910022-0d3f-45ad-86aa-55795ed9e282
dashboardQueries.query:
graph.metadata.vendor_name = "Google"
graph.metadata.product_name = "Google Threat Intelligence"
graph.additional.fields["threat_list_id"] = ""
graph.metadata.entity_type = "DOMAIN_NAME"
$ingestedtime = timestamp.get_timestamp(graph.metadata.collected_timestamp.seconds, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
$verdict = graph.metadata.threat.threat_verdict
$entityid = graph.metadata.product_entity_id
$threatscore = graph.metadata.threat.risk_score
$severity = graph.metadata.threat.severity
$tld = if(graph.additional.fields["tld"]!="", graph.additional.fields["tld"], "-")
$tags = graph.entity.domain.tags
$firstsubmissiontime = timestamp.get_timestamp(graph.entity.domain.creation_time.seconds, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
$detections = graph.metadata.threat.verdict_info.malicious_count
match:
    $entityid, $verdict, $threatscore, $severity, $tld, $firstsubmissiontime, $detections
outcome:
    $time = window.last(cast.as_int($ingestedtime), $ingestedtime)
    $tag = re.replace((arrays.join_string(array_distinct(if($tags!="", $tags, "-")),",")), "(,-)|(-,)","")
order:
    $time desc
limit:
    10000

graph.additional.fields["threat_list_id"] = ""
graph.metadata.entity_type = "IP_ADDRESS"
graph.entity.artifact.tags = /.*vpn.*/ or graph.entity.artifact.tags = /.*tor.*/ or graph.entity.artifact.tags = /.*proxy.*/
$country = graph.entity.artifact.location.country_or_region
$entityid = graph.metadata.product_entity_id
match:
    $country
outcome:
    $count = count_distinct($entityid)
order:
    $count desc
limit:
    10

dashboardQueries.name: b0554a5c-5530-4a21-b10f-20ab80bf16cc
dashboardQueries.query:
graph.metadata.vendor_name = "Google"
graph.metadata.product_name = "Google Threat Intelligence"
graph.additional.fields["threat_list_id"] = ""
graph.metadata.entity_type = "FILE"
graph.metadata.threat.threat_verdict = "MALICIOUS" or graph.metadata.threat.threat_verdict = "SUSPICIOUS"
$entityid = graph.metadata.product_entity_id
outcome:
    $count = count_distinct($entityid)
